Off Calendar Jun-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Andre J. LeLievre, Esq. (916-929-0130; alelievre@nccsinc.com) for Plaintiff Northern California Collection Service, Inc.
Peter Chao, Esq. (510)832-8899; chaolopez@yahoo.com) of Chao and Cowan for Defendant Regional Demolition Inc.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Defendant Regional Demolition Inc:

Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication on its causes of action for open book account and account stated is granted. Plaintiff has maintained its initial burden of production and established that defendant owes $53,252.67. Defendant's opposition fails to create a triable issue of fact. Defendant's only opposing evidence is a declaration of counsel that does not directly contest plaintiff's facts. Defendant cites no authority holding that the Court must adjudicate the complaint and amended cross-complaint together. Defendant's opposing separate statement cites no evidence, does not respond to plaintiff's facts, and does not comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(b)(3). That alone is a basis to grant this motion. Defendant contends that the motion is untimely, but it was e-served on March 10, 2021, 90 days before hearing, well more than the required 75 days notice.

Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Supplemental Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Peter Chao, esq. (510-832-8899) of Chao & Lopez for Defendant, Regional Demolitiom, Inc., et al via courtcall.

No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC.'s Motion For Leave To File A Supplemental Complaint is granted. Section 464 of the Code of Civil Procedure authorizes a plaintiff to file a supplemental complaint alleging facts material to the case occurring after the former complaint. (Code Civ. Proc. 464(a).) The policy of great liberality in permitting amendments of pleadings supports granting the motion. (Flood v. Simpson (1975) 45 Cal.App.3d 644, 647.) That defendant is a suspended corporation that asserts it is in the process of being reinstated is not a reason to deny the motion.

Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
